famous quote:
"I am a product [...of] endless books. My father bought all the books he read and never got rid of any of them. There were books in the study, books in the drawing room, books in the cloakroom, books (two deep) in the great bookcase on the landing, books in a bedroom, books piled as high as my shoulder in the cistern attic, books of all kinds reflecting every transient stage of my parents' interest, books readable and unreadable, books suitable for a child and books most emphatically not. Nothing was forbidden me. In the seemingly endless rainy afternoons I took volume after volume from the shelves. I had always the same certainty of finding a book that was new to me as a man who walks into a field has of finding a new blade of grass."
-- C.S. Lewis
Interview Text:
Gloria's Story:

I sat next to Gloria on a plane back to Colorado for Christmas and we talked the whole time. She told me that she heard God very clearly one time in her life. It was a long time ago when her husband had moved to Georgia to be with her and now she felt he was mad at her for it because it was hard finding a job down there and things were hard. 

"I was lying next to my husband praying about what to do. I would pray each night, but my husband doesn't really pray much. And I heard very clearly, "Who do you think brought you here?"

"It wasn't like a voice in my ears," She motioned to her ears. "I felt it inside." She patted her stomach, still not sure how to describe where she felt it. "It was after I had told everything to God and I stopped speaking. That's when I heard it. It was so peaceful. It was quiet."
Music Video Instructions: Imagine if God's love for us, and ours for him was like this.
